title: ipomoea flavopurpurea
intro: Ipomoea flavopurpurea, also known as Purpleback Morning Glory, is a plant species that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. It is primarily used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and digestive problems. Ipomoea flavopurpurea can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
intro_study: According to "Phytochemistry", ipomoea flavopurpurea does not appear in this study, but a similar flavone compound, Icaritin (ICT), was found to bring various health benefits, including reducing harmful effects on the liver, lung, heart, bone, blood, and skin.
